claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 3 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for UK Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin your Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

The first thing I need to know is about the child (or children) you're claiming for.

**How old is the child you're claiming Child Benefit for?**

Please let me know if they are:
- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

If you have multiple children, we can go through each one. Just let me know their ages and we'll assess each child's eligibility.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, hi! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I've got one child - my little one Alex, who's 3 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Wonderful! Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 3 years old, they fall into the **under 16** category, which is great news as children under 16 automatically qualify for the age requirement.

Let me move to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about Alex's work status. This is a standard question we ask for all children:

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?**

Given that Alex is only 3 years old, I'd expect the answer is **yes** - they're not in paid work. Can you confirm this?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Alex isn't working at all! They're only 3, so they're just at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! That makes perfect sense - Alex is just a little one at home with you. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Next question:

**Is Alex NOT receiving any benefits in their own right?**

This means benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit claimed by the child themselves. Again, given Alex is only 3 years old, I would expect they are not receiving any such benefits. Can you confirm?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's correct - Alex doesn't receive any benefits in their own name. They're too young for that sort of thing.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Exactly right! Let me move on to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next, I need to check about apprenticeships:

**Is Alex currently in an apprenticeship?**

Since Alex is only 3 years old, I'm confident they're not in any kind of apprenticeship. Can you confirm that's correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, definitely not - Alex is only 3! They're not in any apprenticeship or training program.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now we need to check your residency status. 

**Do you meet UK residency requirements?**

You can qualify if **any one** of the following applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. You have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have **pre-settled status** and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3+ continuous months)
4. You have **pre-settled status** and are actively looking for work (can claim for up to 91 days)
5. You have **pre-settled status** with sufficient financial resources (and not claiming certain benefits)
6. You have **pre-settled status** and are studying with sufficient resources
7. You have **pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside
8. You're a **Crown servant** living abroad or in certain qualifying countries

Do any of these apply to you? For most people living in the UK, option 1 (normally living in the UK) will apply.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester. So option 1 applies to me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Living in Manchester means you meet the UK residency requirement. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to check whether you're responsible for Alex.

**Are you responsible for Alex?**

You can qualify if **any one** of the following applies:

1. **You live with Alex** - Alex lives with you in your home
2. **You contribute to Alex's upkeep** - You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent) towards their care (this includes food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents, etc.)
3. **You pay maintenance** - You make maintenance payments under a court order or agreement that cover Alex's upkeep

You mentioned earlier that Alex is "at home with me" - so I assume option 1 applies and Alex lives with you. Is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Alex lives at home with me here in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Lovely! That confirms you're responsible for Alex. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about other potential claimants.

**Is anyone else currently claiming (or planning to claim) Child Benefit for Alex?**

For example:
- Another parent or guardian
- A former partner
- Another family member

Please let me know if:
- **No one else** is claiming for Alex
- **Someone else was claiming**, but you've agreed that you should now receive it instead
- **There's a dispute** about who should claim

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, that keeps things straightforward! Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about Alex's living situation.

**Where is Alex currently living?**

- **At home with you** (normal living situation)
- **In hospital**
- **In local authority care**
- **Being fostered**

Since you mentioned Alex is at home with you in Manchester, I assume it's normal living at home. Is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's correct - Alex is just living at home with me normally. Nothing special like hospital or care.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me proceed with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
We're almost there! One final question:

**Is the local council NOT paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

In other words, are you covering Alex's living costs yourself without financial support from the council specifically for Alex's accommodation or upkeep?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - the council isn't paying anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. I'm covering everything myself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent news! Let me confirm the outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great news! 🎉

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## ✅ You ARE ELIGIBLE for Child Benefit for Alex!

Congratulations! Based on the information you've provided, you meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.

### Summary for Alex:
- ✅ Under 16 years old
- ✅ Not working
- ✅ Not receiving benefits in their own right
- ✅ Not in an apprenticeship
- ✅ You live in the UK
- ✅ You're responsible for Alex (living together)
- ✅ No one else is claiming for Alex
- ✅ Council is not paying for Alex's upkeep

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** (or adoption certificate if applicable)
4. **Payments** are made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

### Important Note:
Be aware of the **High Income Child Benefit Charge** - if you or your partner earns over £60,000 per year, you may have to pay back some or all of the Child Benefit through a tax charge. You can still claim, but it's worth checking if this applies to you.

### Useful Link:
👉 [How to claim Child Benefit](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim)

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements: (1) Age: At 3 years old, Alex is under 16 and automatically qualifies for the age requirement. (2) Work status: Alex is not in paid work (under 24 hours per week requirement met). (3) Benefits: Alex does not receive any benefits in their own right. (4) Apprenticeship: Alex is not in any apprenticeship. (5) Claimant residency: The claimant normally lives in the UK (Manchester). (6) Responsibility: The claimant lives with Alex and is responsible for them. (7) No other claimants: No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. (8) Living situation: Alex lives at home normally (not in hospital, care, or fostered). (9) Council funding: The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 3-year-old child Alex. All eligibility criteria have been met: the child is under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, not in an apprenticeship, and lives at home with the claimant who is responsible for them. The claimant lives in the UK, no one else is claiming for this child, and the council is not contributing to the child's upkeep.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child
